stories.headline: EU leaders meet in Brussels to debate next defense-spending package
stories.summary: European Council members are weighing a proposal that would expand joint defense procurement and accelerate ammunition production. Germany and France are split on the size of the package.

perspectives.keyFraming: European outlets frame this as a test of EU strategic autonomy and a response to changing US security guarantees.
perspectives.summary: Western European coverage emphasizes the proposal would mark the bloc's largest joint defense expenditure to date, with most outlets noting that German fiscal hawks and French industrial advocates remain at odds over the structure and the share of contracts reserved for EU-based manufacturers.

sources.title: EU summit debates landmark defense funding plan
Paris pushes for European preference in defense procurement

perspectives.keyFraming: Wire coverage treats the summit as a procedural milestone with no guaranteed outcome.
perspectives.summary: Centrist wire services report the package is unlikely to be finalized at this meeting, with senior officials describing the day's session as a 'temperature check' before formal drafting begins in June.

sources.title: EU leaders divided on size of joint defense package as summit opens

perspectives.keyFraming: Russian state media frames the meeting as proof of European militarization driven by NATO and US pressure.
perspectives.summary: Russian state outlets describe the package as an escalation that ignores European public sentiment, and they highlight protests in Berlin and Paris against increased defense spending.

sources.title: Brussels accelerates militarization under US pressure, analysts say

stories.headline: ASEAN ministers conclude trade talks with mixed signals on US tariff response
stories.summary: Trade ministers from ten Southeast Asian economies wrapped up two days of talks in Kuala Lumpur. The final communique called for 'open and rules-based trade' without naming the United States.

perspectives.keyFraming: Regional outlets frame the talks as ASEAN navigating between Washington and Beijing without picking sides.
perspectives.summary: Coverage from Southeast Asian outlets emphasizes that smaller member states pushed for stronger language opposing tariffs, while larger economies preferred not to provoke either Washington or Beijing. The communique reflects that compromise.

sources.title: ASEAN treads carefully on tariffs as US-China rivalry deepens
Southeast Asian ministers seek united front on trade

perspectives.keyFraming: Wire coverage focuses on the diplomatic ambiguity of the final statement.
perspectives.summary: Wire services note that the communique did not name any country and that several delegations briefed reporters separately with conflicting accounts of how aggressively ASEAN would respond to new US tariffs.

sources.title: ASEAN avoids naming US in trade statement after closing summit
